L1.area: x86 instruction decoding and disassembly engine

L2.description: An incorrect buffer size calculation in an x86 instruction decoder allows out-of-bounds memory access. An unsafe unchecked access at a slice boundary is used to derive a pointer, which can result in undefined behavior and potential memory corruption when the decoder processes input data.

L3.area: x86 instruction decoder initialization and byte-reading logic within the iced-x86 crate
L3.description: An incorrect buffer size calculation in the x86 instruction decoder's initialization logic allows out-of-bounds memory access. When setting up internal data pointers from an input byte slice, an unsafe unchecked access at the slice's length boundary derives a pointer that may reference memory outside the allocated buffer, leading to undefined behavior and potential memory corruption.

ground_truth.reason: In Decoder::new(), slice.get_unchecked(slice.length()) is used unsafely to create a pointer one byte past the end of the input data slice, resulting in an out-of-bounds memory access and undefined behavior.
